title: HEAT Letter: A zero-parameter derivation of the late-type galaxy size exponent and normalisation from a0(z) = c H(z) / (2 pi)
description: Companion code and LaTeX source for the HEAT (Hubble-Emergent Acceleration Theory) letter. HEAT is a zero-parameter phenomenological model in which the MOND acceleration scale is identified with the surface gravity of the FRW apparent cosmological horizon, a0(z) = c H(z) / (2 pi). This release reproduces the four figures of the letter: (i) the zero-parameter R(z) / R_0 normalisation curve and its matter-era asymptote Om_m^{-1/4}; (ii) the stellar-mass-normalised compactification R_obs / R_0 versus redshift; (iii) the ALMA z ~ 4.5 joint kinematic test (N=4); and (iv) the SPARC 171-galaxy local-anchor chi^2 comparison with constant-a0 MOND. Included are the analysis scripts, the Rotmod_LTG SPARC rotation-curve catalogue, the paper LaTeX source (publication/paper_heat_letter.tex), and the pre-generated figures under heat_output/.
